definitions.subtitle: ={{$parameter["operation"] + ": " + $parameter["resource"]}}
definitions.description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets

builderHint.searchHint: For workflow data storage, DataTable with upsert avoids duplicates. Use Google Sheets when spreadsheet collaboration is specifically needed.

relatedNodes.relationHint: Prefer for workflow data storage with upsert

extraTypeDefContent.content:
<patterns>
<pattern title="autoMapInputData — maps $json fields to sheet columns automatically">
columns: {
  mappingMode: 'autoMapInputData',
  value: {},
  schema: [
    { id: 'Name', displayName: 'Name', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: true },
    { id: 'Email', displayName: 'Email', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: false }
  ]
}
</pattern>
<pattern title="defineBelow — explicit expression mapping">
columns: {
  mappingMode: 'defineBelow',
  value: { name: expr('{{ $json.name }}'), email: expr('{{ $json.email }}') },
  schema: [
    { id: 'name', displayName: 'name', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: true },
    { id: 'email', displayName: 'email', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: true }
  ]
}
</pattern>
</patterns>

hints.message: Use the 'Minimise API Calls' option for greater efficiency if your sheet is uniformly formatted without gaps between columns or rows
No columns found in Google Sheet. All rows will be appended
Note on using an expression for Sheet: It will be evaluated only once, so all items will use the same sheet. It will be calculated by evaluating the expression for the first input item.
Note on using an expression for Document: It will be evaluated only once, so all items will use the same document. It will be calculated by evaluating the expression for the first input item.

options.name: Append or Update Row
options.description: Append a new row or update an existing one (upsert)
options.action: Append or update row in sheet

options.name: Append Row
options.description: Create a new row in a sheet

options.name: Clear
options.description: Delete all the contents or a part of a sheet

options.name: Create
options.description: Create a new sheet

options.name: Delete
options.description: Permanently delete a sheet

options.name: Delete Rows or Columns
options.description: Delete columns or rows from a sheet
options.action: Delete rows or columns from sheet

options.name: Get Row(s)
options.description: Retrieve one or more rows from a sheet

options.name: Update Row
options.description: Update an existing row in a sheet

builderHint.propertyHint: Default to mode: 'list' which is easier for users to set up. Never invent a spreadsheet ID: when no credential or explored resource provides a real one, use mode: 'id' with an empty value and a cachedResultName placeholder. Resource locator value must be `{ __rl: true, mode, value }` — never a plain string or `expr()` wrapper.

properties.errorMessage: Not a valid Google Drive File URL
Not a valid Google Drive File ID

builderHint.propertyHint: Default to mode: 'list' when you have a real numeric sheet ID (gid) from explored resources. If you only know the sheet by its name, use mode: 'name' with that name — a title placed in list or id mode cannot resolve. Resource locator value must be `{ __rl: true, mode, value }` — never a plain string or `expr()` wrapper.

properties.name: dataMode

options.name: Auto-Map Input Data to Columns
options.description: Use when node input properties match destination column names

options.name: Map Each Column Below
options.description: Set the value for each destination column

options.name: Nothing
options.description: Do not send anything

properties.description: Whether to insert the input data this node receives in the new row

properties.displayName: In this mode, make sure the incoming data is named the same as the columns in your Sheet. (Use an 'Edit Fields' node before this node to change it if required.)
properties.name: autoMapNotice

values.name: fieldId
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

builderHint.propertyHint: Pass the full resourceMapper object: { mappingMode, value, schema }. A bare string like 'autoMapInputData' fails validation. `append` is plain insert — do NOT add `matchingColumns` here (that is for the `appendOrUpdate` and `update` operations).

options.name: cellFormat
Let Google Sheets format
options.description: Cells are styled as if you typed the values into Google Sheets directly

options.name: Let n8n format
options.description: Cells have the same types as the input data
Determines how data should be interpreted

values.name: headerRow
values.description: Index of the row which contains the keys. Starts at 1. The incoming node data is matched to the keys for assignment. The matching is case sensitive.

options.name: handlingExtraData
Insert in New Column(s)
options.description: Create a new column for extra data

options.name: Ignore Them
options.description: Ignore extra data

options.name: Error
options.description: Throw an error
What do to with fields that don't match any columns in the Google Sheet

options.name: handlingExtraData
Insert in New Column(s)
options.description: Create a new column for extra data

options.name: Ignore Them
options.description: Ignore extra data

options.name: Error
options.description: Throw an error
What do to with fields that don't match any columns in the Google Sheet

options.name: useAppend
options.hint: Use if your sheet has no gaps between rows or columns
options.description: Whether to use append instead of update(default), this is more efficient but in some cases data might be misaligned

properties.name: clear
properties.description: What to clear

properties.name: startIndex
properties.description: The row number to delete from, The first row is 1

properties.name: startIndex
properties.description: The column to delete

properties.name: range
properties.description: The table range to read from or to append data to. See the Google documentation for the details. If it contains multiple sheets it can also be added like this: "MySheet!A:F"

properties.name: title
properties.description: The name of the sheet

options.name: hidden
options.description: Whether the sheet is hidden in the UI, false if it's visible

options.name: rightToLeft
options.description: Whether the sheet is an RTL sheet instead of an LTR sheet

options.name: sheetId
options.description: The ID of the sheet. Must be non-negative. This field cannot be changed once set.

options.name: index
options.description: The index of the sheet within the spreadsheet

options.name: tabColor
options.description: The color of the tab in the UI

properties.name: toDelete

options.name: Rows
options.description: Rows to delete

options.name: Columns
options.description: Columns to delete

properties.description: What to delete

properties.name: startIndex
properties.description: The row number to delete from, The first row is 2

properties.name: startIndex
properties.description: The column to delete

values.name: lookupColumn
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: lookupValue
values.hint: The column must have this value to be matched

properties.name: combineFilters
properties.description: How to combine the conditions defined in "Filters": AND requires all conditions to be true, OR requires at least one condition to be true

options.name: AND
options.description: Only rows that meet all the conditions are selected

options.name: OR
options.description: Rows that meet at least one condition are selected

properties.name: combineFilters
properties.description: How to combine the conditions defined in "Filters": AND requires all conditions to be true, OR requires at least one condition to be true

options.name: AND
options.description: Only rows that meet all the conditions are selected

options.name: OR
options.description: Rows that meet at least one condition are selected

options.name: Detect Automatically
options.description: Automatically detect the data range

options.name: Specify Range (A1 Notation)
options.description: Manually specify the data range

options.name: Specify Range (Rows)
options.description: Manually specify the data range

values.name: headerRow
values.description: Index is relative to the set 'Range', first row index is 1
values.hint: Index of the row which contains the column names

values.name: firstDataRow
values.description: Index is relative to the set 'Range', first row index is 1
values.hint: Index of first row which contains the actual data

values.name: range
values.description: The table range to read from or to append data to. See the Google documentation for the details.
values.hint: You can specify both the rows and the columns, e.g. C4:E7

values.name: general

options.name: Values (unformatted)
options.description: Numbers stay as numbers, but any currency signs or special formatting is lost

options.name: Values (formatted)
options.description: Numbers are turned to text, and displayed as in Google Sheets (e.g. with commas or currency signs)

values.description: Determines how values should be rendered in the output

options.name: Formatted Text
options.description: As displayed in Google Sheets, e.g. '01/01/2022'

options.name: Serial Number
options.description: A number representing the number of days since Dec 30, 1899

options.name: returnFirstMatch
options.description: Whether to select the first row of the sheet or the first matching row (if filters are set)

options.name: returnAllMatches
Return First Match
options.description: Return only the first match

options.name: Return All Matches
options.description: Return all values that match
By default only the first result gets returned, Set to "Return All Matches" to get multiple matches

properties.name: dataMode

options.name: Auto-Map Input Data to Columns
options.description: Use when node input properties match destination column names

options.name: Map Each Column Below
options.description: Set the value for each destination column

options.name: Nothing
options.description: Do not send anything

properties.description: Whether to insert the input data this node receives in the new row

properties.name: columnToMatchOn
properties.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression
properties.hint: Used to find the correct row to update. Doesn't get changed.

properties.displayName: Value of Column to Match On
properties.name: valueToMatchOn

values.name: column
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

builderHint.propertyHint: Pass the full resourceMapper object: { mappingMode, value, schema, matchingColumns }. `matchingColumns` is REQUIRED for this operation — it must be a non-empty `string[]` of header names that uniquely identify the row to update; without it the node throws 'Could not get parameter' at runtime. Use the `append` operation instead if there is no key column to match on. A bare string like 'autoMapInputData' silently fails validation; always send the full resourceMapper object.

Pass the full resourceMapper object: { mappingMode, value, schema, matchingColumns }. `matchingColumns` is REQUIRED for this operation — it must be a non-empty `string[]` of header names that uniquely identify the row to update; without it the node throws 'Could not get parameter' at runtime. Use the `append` operation instead if there is no key column to match on. A bare string like 'autoMapInputData' silently fails validation; always send the full resourceMapper object.

definitions.name: n8n-nodes-base.googleSheets

definitions.subtitle: ={{$parameter["operation"] + ": " + $parameter["resource"]}}
definitions.description: Read, update and write data to Google Sheets

builderHint.searchHint: For workflow data storage, DataTable with upsert avoids duplicates. Use Google Sheets when spreadsheet collaboration is specifically needed.

relatedNodes.relationHint: Prefer for workflow data storage with upsert

extraTypeDefContent.content:
<patterns>
<pattern title="autoMapInputData — maps $json fields to sheet columns automatically">
columns: {
  mappingMode: 'autoMapInputData',
  value: {},
  schema: [
    { id: 'Name', displayName: 'Name', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: true },
    { id: 'Email', displayName: 'Email', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: false }
  ]
}
</pattern>
<pattern title="defineBelow — explicit expression mapping">
columns: {
  mappingMode: 'defineBelow',
  value: { name: expr('{{ $json.name }}'), email: expr('{{ $json.email }}') },
  schema: [
    { id: 'name', displayName: 'name', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: true },
    { id: 'email', displayName: 'email', required: false, defaultMatch: false, display: true, type: 'string', canBeUsedToMatch: true }
  ]
}
</pattern>
</patterns>

properties.displayName: New node version available: get the latest version with added features from the nodes panel.
properties.name: oldVersionNotice

options.name: Append
options.description: Append data to a sheet

options.name: Clear
options.description: Clear data from a sheet

options.name: Create
options.description: Create a new sheet

options.name: Create or Update
options.description: Create a new record, or update the current one if it already exists (upsert)

options.name: Delete
options.description: Delete columns and rows from a sheet

options.name: Lookup
options.description: Look up a specific column value and return the matching row
options.action: Look up a column value in a sheet

options.name: Read
options.description: Read data from a sheet

options.name: Remove
options.description: Remove a sheet

options.name: Update
options.description: Update rows in a sheet

properties.name: sheetId
properties.description: The ID of the Google Spreadsheet. Found as part of the sheet URL https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{ID}/.

properties.name: range
properties.description: The table range to read from or to append data to. See the Google documentation for the details. If it contains multiple sheets it can also be added like this: "MySheet!A:F"

properties.name: toDelete
properties.description: Deletes columns and rows from a sheet

values.name: sheetId
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: startIndex
values.description: The start index (0 based and inclusive) of column to delete

values.name: amount
values.description: Number of columns to delete

values.name: sheetId
values.description: Choose from the list, or specify an ID using an expression

values.name: startIndex
values.description: The start index (0 based and inclusive) of row to delete

values.name: amount
values.description: Number of rows to delete

properties.name: rawData
properties.description: Whether the data should be returned RAW instead of parsed into keys according to their header

properties.name: dataProperty
properties.description: The name of the property into which to write the RAW data

properties.name: rawData
properties.description: Whether the data supplied is RAW instead of parsed into keys

properties.name: dataProperty
properties.description: The name of the property from which to read the RAW data

properties.name: dataStartRow
properties.description: Index of the first row which contains the actual data and not the keys. Starts with 0.

properties.name: keyRow
properties.description: Index of the row which contains the keys. Starts at 0. The incoming node data is matched to the keys for assignment. The matching is case sensitive.

properties.name: lookupColumn
properties.description: The name of the column in which to look for value

properties.name: lookupValue
properties.description: The value to look for in column

properties.name: key
properties.description: The name of the key to identify which data should be updated in the sheet

options.name: continue
options.description: By default, the workflow stops executing if the lookup/read does not return values

options.name: returnAllMatches
options.description: By default only the first result gets returned. If options gets set all found matches get returned.

options.displayName: Use Header Names as JSON Paths
options.name: usePathForKeyRow
options.description: Whether you want to match the headers as path, for example, the row header "category.name" will match the "category" object and get the field "name" from it. By default "category.name" will match with the field with exact name, not nested object.

options.name: valueInputMode
RAW
options.description: The values will not be parsed and will be stored as-is

options.name: User Entered
options.description: The values will be parsed as if the user typed them into the UI. Numbers will stay as numbers, but strings may be converted to numbers, dates, etc. following the same rules that are applied when entering text into a cell via the Google Sheets UI.
Determines how data should be interpreted

options.name: valueRenderMode
Formatted Value
options.description: Values will be calculated & formatted in the reply according to the cell's formatting.Formatting is based on the spreadsheet's locale, not the requesting user's locale.For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return "$1.23"

options.name: Formula
options.description: Values will not be calculated. The reply will include the formulas. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return "=A1".

options.name: Unformatted Value
options.description: Values will be calculated, but not formatted in the reply. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return the number 1.23.
Determines how values should be rendered in the output

options.name: valueRenderMode
Formatted Value
options.description: Values will be calculated & formatted in the reply according to the cell's formatting.Formatting is based on the spreadsheet's locale, not the requesting user's locale. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return "$1.23".

options.name: Formula
options.description: Values will not be calculated. The reply will include the formulas. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return "=A1".

options.name: Unformatted Value
options.description: Values will be calculated, but not formatted in the reply. For example, if A1 is 1.23 and A2 is =A1 and formatted as currency, then A2 would return the number 1.23.
Determines how values should be rendered in the output

  • fil (639-2 if no 639-1 format exists)

columnCount

options.name: columnGroupControlAfter
options.description: Whether the column grouping control toggle is shown after the group

options.name: frozenRowCount
options.description: The number of rows that are frozen in the grid

options.name: hideGridlines
options.description: Whether the grid isn't showing gridlines in the UI

options.name: rowCount
options.description: The number of rows in the grid

options.name: rowGroupControlAfter
options.description: Whether the row grouping control toggle is shown after the group
The type of the sheet
